question: A junior technician is reviewing a support ticket. The clue says: "Layer 1 moves raw bits over media and includes signaling, cabling, radio, and connector concerns." Which networking concept is being described? [Scenario 1]
explanation: Troubleshooting a damaged copper cable belongs at OSI Layer 1 because the medium itself is failing.

question: A junior technician is reviewing a support ticket. The clue says: "TCP is connection-oriented and uses sequencing, acknowledgments, and retransmission." Which networking concept is being described? [Scenario 101]

question: A help desk technician sees this description in a knowledge-base note: "UDP is connectionless and avoids reliability overhead for latency-sensitive traffic." Which term best matches it? [Scenario 102]

question: A learner is mapping Cisco CCST Networking concepts to support tasks. Which option matches this description: "DNS resolves names to IP addresses so people can use hostnames instead of numeric addresses."? [Scenario 103]

question: A network support ticket includes this finding: "DHCP automatically leases IP settings such as address, mask, gateway, and DNS servers." What should the technician identify it as? [Scenario 104]

question: A help desk technician sees this description in a knowledge-base note: "SSH provides encrypted remote command-line administration, normally on TCP port 22." Which term best matches it? [Scenario 106]

question: A learner wants to identify two concepts that belong to Standards and Concepts. Which two choices fit this objective area? (Select two.) [Scenario 107]

question: A learner wants to identify two concepts that belong to Standards and Concepts. Which two choices fit this objective area? (Select two.) [Scenario 108]

question: Match each standards and concepts term to the best description. [Scenario 109]
itemsRight: Layer 1 moves raw bits over media and includes signaling, cabling, radio, and connector concerns.

OSI network layer: Routers make forwarding decisions with IP addresses, so routing belongs to the network layer.
